summary: Completed 26 pairwise regression tests for the Self absence creation flow using the school administrator account (schooladmin@mailinator.com). All 26 tests passed.

content.text: Pairwise regression coverage for the Self absence creation flow, validating that an administrator can create absences for themselves across every reason, duration type, and substitute preference. The run confirms the form, leave balance deductions, and sub picker all behave correctly in Self mode.

Environment: InstaSub Staging — InstaSub Account:  (School Administrator role) Flow: Self absence creation (/absence/createAbsence, Self radio selected) Sub selected (Notify 1 Sub / Assign a specific sub): Sub 1

6 reasons: Sick, Personal, Vacation, Illness-Family Member, Educational Leave, Professional Development 5 duration types: Full Day, Half Day AM, Half Day PM, Enter Time Manually (10:00–15:00), Add Class 5 sub preferences: Notify all subs, Notify 1 Sub, Notify my favorites, Assign a specific sub, No sub required
Add Class replaces the Duration dropdown. The class time inputs define the absence window.

26/26 tests passed. All 6 reasons, all 5 duration types (including Add Class), and all 5 sub preferences exercised. Sub 2 was successfully selected for all Notify 1 Sub and assigned a specific sub case.
Sub picker behaviour: In the Self flow, the sub picker is available once a start and end date are set. Sub selection worked as expected for all applicable tests.

All 6 absence reasons selectable via the Self flow All 4 standard duration types (Full Day, Half Day AM, Half Day PM, Enter Time Manually) work in Self mode Add Class flow works for all 6 reasons in Self mode All 5 sub preference options available and functional in Self mode Sub 2 selectable via Notify 1 Sub and Assign a specific sub Leave balance deducted correctly after each absence creation No blocking errors or UI failures across the 26-test run
